examples.title: MVC Connection Pseudocode
examples.description: Demonstrates the basic flow of the MVC pattern
examples.code:
BEGIN
 User sends HTTP Request to Controller
 Controller invokes Model method
 Model returns Data to Controller
 Controller forwards request with Model data to View
 View renders UI using Model data
 View returns HTML Response to User
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode illustrates the basic flow of the MVC pattern, where the User sends a request to the Controller, which then interacts with the Model to retrieve data, and finally forwards the request to the View to render the UI.

examples.title: MVC Connection Java Implementation
examples.description: Demonstrates the MVC pattern using Java Servlets and JSPs
examples.explanation: This Java code demonstrates the Controller's role in the MVC pattern, where it invokes the Model method to retrieve data and then forwards the request to the View to render the UI.

examples.title: MVC Connection JSP Implementation
examples.description: Demonstrates the View's role in the MVC pattern using JSPs
examples.code:
<%@ page contentType="text/html" %> <%@ page import="java.util.List" %> <%@ page import="com.example.Student" %> List students = (List) request.getAttribute("students"); <% for (Student student : students) { %> <% } %>
Name Age
<%= student.getName() %> <%= student.getAge() %>
examples.explanation: This JSP code demonstrates the View's role in the MVC pattern, where it retrieves the Model data from the request attribute and renders the UI using that data.
